Field Identification Key · 24 Species

Salamanders of Louisiana

Interactive dichotomous key — answer questions step by step to identify any salamander you encounter

Identify Your Salamander

This key guides you through a series of binary choices — each question narrows down the possibilities until you reach a species identification. Louisiana has 24 salamander species ranging from tiny lungless plethodontids to giant amphiumas exceeding three feet. Observe limb size, toe count, skin texture, and pattern carefully before starting.

All Louisiana salamanders are native. No introduced species are established. Several species (amphiumas, sirens) are aquatic and may be encountered by fishermen or in traps. All salamanders are harmless to handle, though some produce mildly toxic skin secretions.
